name: Patricia & Harold Bennett
tagline: Retired couple with estate planning focus
description: A retired couple in their early 70s in Tunbridge Wells. Both drawing Defined Benefit pensions from public sector careers (NHS and Civil Service). No debt, substantial savings, and active gifting to grandchildren. Key focus on Inheritance Tax planning.

family_members.notes: Son, married, lives in London with 2 children
Daughter, married, lives in Brighton with 3 children

properties.address: 15 Camden Road, Tunbridge Wells, Kent, TN1 2QE
properties.notes: Mortgage paid off since 2010. Family home for 30 years.

savings_accounts.notes: Main household account for pension income and bills

db_pensions.notes: Patricia's NHS pension - retired at 60. 30 years service. 50% spouse benefit on death. Index-linked to CPI.
Harold's HMRC Civil Service pension - retired at 65. 35 years service. 50% spouse benefit on death. Index-linked to CPI.

state_pension.notes: Patricia receiving full new state pension

spouse_state_pension.notes: Harold receiving full new state pension

gifts.notes: Patricia's annual exemption 2024/25 - to Emma, Thomas, Lucy Junior ISAs
Harold's annual exemption 2024/25 - to Sophie, William Junior ISAs
Patricia's annual exemption 2025/26 - to Emma, Thomas, Lucy Junior ISAs
Harold's annual exemption 2025/26 - to Sophie, William Junior ISAs

chattels.notes: Georgian sideboard and Victorian dining set

iht_profile.notes: On first death, assets transfer to spouse tax-free. On second death, both Nil Rate Bands and Residence Nil Rate Bands available (up to £1M threshold). Current combined estate ~£845k so Inheritance Tax exposure ~£138k on second death.

key_concerns: Will our pensions keep up with inflation?
How much Inheritance Tax will our children pay?
Should we be gifting more now while we can enjoy seeing them use it?
What happens if one of us needs care?
Are we protected if pension rules change under new government policies?
Should we consider equity release or is that a last resort?

highlights.primary_focus: Inheritance Tax planning, gifting strategy, care cost planning

risk_profile.notes: Retired couple focused on capital preservation and income generation. Cautious approach with DB pension income security.

will.executor_notes: Mirror wills in place. On first death, everything to surviving spouse. On second death, estate split equally between Mark and Susan. Specific legacies to grandchildren for education.

bequests.conditions: To be divided equally between grandchildren for education costs when they reach 18

goals.description: Use both annual exemptions (£6,000 total) to gift to grandchildren Junior ISAs, reducing estate for Inheritance Tax
Build education fund across 5 grandchildren's Junior ISAs for university costs
Maintain liquid savings to cover potential care costs without selling home. Target 3 years residential care at current rates.
Reduce estate below combined Nil Rate Band + Residence Nil Rate Band threshold (£1M) through strategic gifting over 7 years
Annual family gathering holiday - cruise or European trip with children and grandchildren

letter_to_spouse.immediate_actions:
1. Contact Mark immediately - he is co-executor with Adams & Co
2. Contact Adams & Co Solicitors (01892 523 456)
3. Access joint Nationwide account for immediate expenses
4. Contact AJ Bell about investment accounts
5. Notify NHS Pensions about my death (survivor pension applies)
6. Keep my phone active for account verifications
7. Register the death at Tunbridge Wells Register Office
8. Obtain at least 10 death certificates

letter_to_spouse.funeral_service_details: Cremation with service at St John's Church, Tunbridge Wells. Reception at home (The Willows) if weather permits, otherwise the church hall. Traditional Church of England service. Hymns: 'How Great Thou Art', 'Morning Has Broken'. Donations to NHS Charities Together (for my nursing colleagues).
letter_to_spouse.obituary_wishes: Mention my 30 years nursing career with the NHS, rising to Nurse Manager. Note Harold as my devoted husband of 50 years. Mention Mark and Susan, and our five wonderful grandchildren. Include my South African heritage and how we built our life together in England.
letter_to_spouse.additional_wishes:
My dearest Harold,

We've had 50 wonderful years together. Please don't be alone - the children will look after you and the grandchildren need their Grandpa.

To Mark and Susan - look after your father. Make sure he eats properly and stays active. Don't let him become a hermit!

To our five grandchildren - Grandma loves you all so much. The money we've saved for your education is to help you follow your dreams. Work hard at school but remember to have fun too.

Harold - maybe it's finally time for that garden room extension you've always talked about. You have my blessing!

All my love forever,
Patricia xxx

Using both annual exemptions (£3,000 each).

Continue gifting to reduce estate for IHT.

additional_boxes.title: Inheritance Tax Planning Status
additional_boxes.content:
Combined estate: ~£845,000

Allowances on second death:
- NRB: £650,000 (double)
- RNRB: £350,000 (double - house to children)
- Total: £1,000,000

Currently BELOW threshold - no IHT due!

Gifting history:
- Mark: £20,000 (April 2022)
- Susan: £20,000 (June 2021)
- Grandchildren: £6,000/year ongoing

All gifts within 7 years recorded with Adams & Co.

additional_boxes.title: Care Cost Planning
additional_boxes.content:
Care Fee Reserve: ~£145,500 in savings

Target: 3 years residential care (£180k)

LPA in place - Mark and Susan are attorneys for both of us.

Preferred care homes (researched):
- Bowes House, Tunbridge Wells
- The Cedars, Southborough

Avoid selling The Willows for care if possible.
Deferred payment agreement available from council.

spouse_letter.funeral_service_details: Cremation with service at St John's Church. Simple reception at home. Traditional hymns. Donations to British Heart Foundation (for the bypass that gave me 20 more years).
spouse_letter.obituary_wishes: Mention my 35 years in the Civil Service at HMRC, my dedication to public service, Patricia as my beloved wife, and our children and grandchildren.
spouse_letter.additional_wishes:
My dearest Patricia,

Thank you for 50 years of love and partnership. You've been my rock.

Look after yourself. The children will take care of you. Maybe move closer to Susan in Brighton - the grandchildren would love having you nearby.

To Mark and Susan - your mother devoted her life to caring for others. Now it's time to care for her.

To my grandchildren - Grandpa is so proud of all of you. Work hard, be kind, and look after each other.

All my love,
Harold

life_events.description: Family gathering and celebration for Harold's 70th birthday
50th wedding anniversary cruise to the Mediterranean - a dream trip
Anticipated need for part-time home care assistance as we age
Wedding gifts for grandchildren as they marry - £5,000 each for 4 grandchildren
Final car replacement - automatic, easy to access vehicle
Update kitchen and bathroom for accessibility and modern conveniences
Using annual exemption to gift to children and grandchildren each year
